projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

observations.summary: The front porch and backyard area are empty and quiet under a clear sky. No people or animals are visible in the scene.
The dog run is currently empty. A single tennis ball sits on the artificial grass near the wooden fence.

Roxanne's black Porsche Cayenne is parked in the driveway, and an unknown black car is parked on the street. The camera feed has encountered a significant error, with several consecutive frames showing no video being received.

insights.summary: [front_yard] The front_yard camera is experiencing a loss of signal, displaying 'No frames have been received' error messages.
[dog_run] The 'dog_run' camera is reporting a loss of signal or frame reception error, which may indicate a connection or hardware issue.
[doorbell] Urgent dog barking detected at the dog_run.
[front_yard] The camera feed experienced a temporary loss of frames.

Despite all cameras showing the house as empty and quiet since 11:38 AM, there is a rapid and sustained burst of unclassified audio starting at 11:43 AM, including a doorbell chime and multiple audio events in the dog run and backyard. This suggests a recent, unobserved event or visitor occurred just before the current time, which the cameras failed to capture.
[dog_run] The camera stream experienced a momentary interruption/error.
[front_yard] The camera feed is displaying a 'No frames have been received' error message, indicating a potential issue with the camera's connection or streaming capability.

There is a significant contradiction between the '11 mobile devices home' presence signal at 10:48 AM and the camera feeds, which consistently show the entire house and yard empty and quiet. This suggests the occupancy count may be inaccurate or that the family is present but completely out of view, requiring a manual check of the home's status.
[doorbell] A smoke or carbon monoxide alarm is beeping at the front door.
[dog_run] The dog_run camera is not receiving frames; check error logs for potential connectivity or hardware issues.
